English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Thomas Henick-Kling papers
Henick-Kling, Thomas.
22-2-3604
3 cubic feet. 3 cubic feet.
1961-2002. Correspondence, Publications, Research Materials Collection consits of papers from his work at the NYSAES in Geneva, NY. Most are related to wine and grapes. Areas covered include New York, California, Germany, and others. Contains research materials, presentations, brochures, and slides. Collection material in English 6068848
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
November 2007
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, Evan Fay Earle, November 2007
Date modified:
Fredrika Loew, October 2018
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Henick-Kling served as a professor in the department of food science and technology at the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, NY. Other posts include director of the National Wine and Grape Industry Centre at Charles Stuart University in New South Wales, Australia and at Cornell University, he directed the Wine Laboratory and Data Bank and organized the annual Wine Industry Workshop.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Collection consits of papers from his work at the NYSAES in Geneva, NY. Most are related to wine and grapes. Areas covered include New York, California, Germany, and others. Contains research materials, presentations, brochures, and slides.
RELATED MATERIALS
Forms part of the Eastern Wine and Grape Archive. (EWGA)
Cite As:
Thomas Henick-Kling papers. #22-2-3604. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Grapes. Viticulture. Wine and wine making. Viticulture -- New York (State). Grape industry -- New York (State). Viticulture -- Europe. New York State Agricultural Experiment Station
